content:
{
  "idea": "Cross-Modal EEG/MEG/ECoG Foundation Model for Plug-and-Play BCI",
  "idea_description": "Build a self-supervised neural foundation model trained across EEG, MEG, ECoG, movement, and speech data from multiple subjects and days. The model will learn shared representations that account for signal drift across sessions, tasks, and individuals while requiring minimal patient-specific calibration. This cross-modal foundation model will be pretrained on unlabeled multimodal neurophysiological and behavioral data, then fine-tuned or adapted to new patients for calibrated motor and speech decoding. The model will use contrastive representation learning and task-agnostic pretext objectives to align modalities and reduce calibration burden.",
  "technical_plan": {
    "target_problem": "Session-, task-, and person-dependent signal drift in noninvasive neurophysiological BCI signals with sparse labels, preventing plug-and-play motor and speech decoding.",
    "core_method": "Self-supervised cross-modal foundation modeling with contrastive neural representations trained on EEG, MEG, ECoG, movement, and speech data across subjects and days.",
    "limitation_addressed": "Sparse labels, signal drift across sessions, tasks, and people, and the need for extensive patient-specific calibration in plug-and-play BCI.",
    "expected_result": "A foundation model that enables calibrated decoding of motor and speech signals with substantially less patient-specific calibration than traditional BCI pipelines."
  },
  "lineage_connection": {
    "source_ideas": [
      "Contrastive Representation Learning for EEG Foundation Modeling",
      "Multimodal Neural Representation Learning across EEG, MEG, and Behavior",
      "Plug-and-Play BCI: Moving Beyond Patient-Specific Calibration"
    ],
    "reused_parts": [
      "Contrastive representation learning for neural signal alignment",
      "Multimodal neurophysiological and behavioral data integration",
      "Patient-specific calibration reduction via pretrained decoder bases"
    ],
    "changed_parts": [
      "Extending the foundation model to include ECoG and movement/speech signals across multiple modalities and sessions",
      "Adding task-agnostic pretext objectives for signal drift and calibration robustness"
    ],
    "why_next_step": "This follows from the frontier question by combining EEG foundation modeling with self-supervised cross-modal alignment to address sparse labels and drift in plug-and-play BCI.",
    "lineage_risk": "The proposed model may fail if cross-modal contrastive learning cannot effectively align EEG, MEG, ECoG, movement, and speech signals across subjects and days."
  },
  "evaluation_plan": {
    "setup": "Evaluate on multi-session, multi-subject motor and speech BCI benchmark datasets with held-out sessions and sparse labels. Compare pretrained foundation model fine-tuning against patient-specific calibration baselines and zero-shot transfer baselines.",
    "baselines": [
      "Patient-specific BCI calibration with full calibration data",
      "Cross-session transfer without foundation modeling",
      "Unimodal EEG/MEG/Speech decoding only"
    ],
    "metrics": [
      "Decoding accuracy for motor and speech tasks",
      "Calibration burden measured by required calibration samples",
      "Signal drift robustness across sessions and subjects",
      "Cross-modal alignment consistency via representation similarity"
    ],
    "risks": [
      "The foundation model may overfit to training session/task distributions and fail to generalize to held-out data.",
      "Sparse labels may limit pretext objective supervision and prevent robust cross-modal representation learning.",
      "ECoG and movement/speech signals may have different spatial/temporal characteristics that complicate alignment."
    ]
  }
}
prompt:
You are a senior researcher in Neuroscience / Brain-Computer Interface.
Based on your expertise, generate a new research idea for the following frontier question.

**Frontier Question**:
Large Cognition Model explores EEG foundation modeling, but plug-and-play BCI remains unsolved because noninvasive signals drift across sessions, tasks, and people and labels are sparse. What self-supervised neural foundation model would align EEG, MEG, ECoG, movement, and speech data across subjects and days to enable calibrated motor and speech decoding with minimal patient-specific calibration?

Generate a new research idea for the frontier question.

Return ONLY valid JSON. Do not wrap it in Markdown fences.
{
  "idea": "<short name of the proposed idea, 5-15 words>",
  "idea_description": "<150-250 words describing the full research idea>",
  "technical_plan": {
    "target_problem": "<specific problem, dataset, population, or phenomenon>",
    "core_method": "<technical method or mechanism, not a vague goal>",
    "limitation_addressed": "<frontier limitation repaired by this idea>",
    "expected_result": "<scientific result or claim if successful>"
  },
  "lineage_connection": {
    "source_ideas": ["<paper title or source idea used by this proposal>", "..."],
    "reused_parts": ["<specific method, evidence, data, or problem framing reused from the sources>", "..."],
    "changed_parts": ["<specific modified, replaced, combined, or newly added part>", "..."],
    "why_next_step": "<why this idea follows from the provided context>",
    "lineage_risk": "<most likely way this source connection could be wrong>"
  },
  "evaluation_plan": {
    "setup": "<concrete experiment, benchmark, proof, or analysis setup>",
    "baselines": ["<baseline>", "..."],
    "metrics": ["<metric>", "..."],
    "risks": ["<risk>", "..."]
  }
}

metadata.system_prompt: You are a scientific lineage analyst and idea generator. Respond with a single JSON object inside ```json ... ``` fences matching the schema requested in the user prompt. No commentary outside the JSON.
